title: Perfect state transfer in Grover walks on normal Cayley graphs
abstract: A Cayley graph $\operatorname{Cay}(Γ,S)$ over a finite group $Γ$ is said to be normal if its connection set $S$ is a union of some conjugacy classes of $Γ$. This paper investigates perfect state transfer in Grover walks on normal Cayley graphs. The Grover walk is a widely studied discrete-time quantum walk. We establish a necessary and sufficient condition for the occurrence of perfect state transfer on normal Cayley graphs. As applications, we derive explicit spectral criteria for perfect state transfer on Cayley graphs over abelian groups, dicyclic groups, and dihedral groups. These results yield several infinite families of Cayley graphs exhibiting perfect state transfer. We further obtain simple combinatorial characterizations of the existence of perfect state transfer on Cayley graphs over dihedral and dicyclic groups. Our general characterization also recovers a number of previously known results as special cases. As a further consequence, we obtain a complete characterization of perfect state transfer on unitary Cayley graphs. In particular, we prove that exactly four graphs in the class of unitary Cayley graphs exhibit perfect state transfer.

bite.hook: Mathematicians proved that exactly four unitary Cayley graphs support perfect state transfer in Grover walks.
tldr: Analyzed conditions required for perfect quantum state transfer in Grover walks on normal Cayley graphs.
Derived theoretical spectral criteria across abelian, dicyclic, and dihedral graph structures.
Proved that exactly four graphs within the unitary Cayley family allow perfect state transfer.
bite.whyItMatters: Perfect state transfer in quantum walks is vital for moving quantum information across quantum network topologies without loss.
bite.eli5: Imagine passing a secret note through a maze of connected rooms so that it arrives with 100% certainty. Mathematicians figured out the exact shape rules of these mazes where quantum particles can pass notes perfectly.

keyNumbers.label: Unitary Cayley graphs with PST
keyNumbers.context: Exact number of graphs in the unitary Cayley class exhibiting perfect state transfer.

glossary.shortDef: The exact transmission of a quantum state between distant nodes in a network with unit probability.
glossary.eli5Def: Sending quantum information across a network with zero data loss.

glossary.shortDef: A discrete-time quantum walk model using the Grover coin operator.
glossary.eli5Def: A quantum version of stepping randomly through a network, using quantum trickery to explore faster.

glossary.shortDef: A graph encoding the algebraic structure of a group generated by a specified set.
glossary.eli5Def: A map where locations and paths are defined by mathematical group symmetry rules.
